expectations.text: Refuses to accept the impl edit as valid user work
expectations.evidence: 'This is a common anti-pattern sometimes called "test-chasing" or "gaming the tests." The tests went green, but the code got worse, not better.'

expectations.text: Instructs user to revert impl (mentions git restore or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: 'Let's revert validate.go back to the scheme-checking version' + code block of reverted code

expectations.text: Reminds user their job is writing tests, not impl
expectations.evidence: Does not frame it as the user's job being tests specifically. Instead discusses generic TDD loop. Missing the 'user_writes=test' framing.

expectations.text: Mentions the give-up escape for this TODO if impl is genuinely wrong
expectations.evidence: No skill-specific give-up / gave_up concept mentioned. Only generic 'question whether test or impl is wrong'.
